text.content:
A Beautiful Mine

Quest available in Termalaine. Reward: 50 gp from Speaker Masthew.

The Hook

Darmo Mazlu, a human boy standing on a box in the street, cries out the news: "The gemstone mine is closed because of monsters! Speaker Masthew is offering fifty gold to anyone who clears it out!"

The boy directs interested parties to Speaker Oarus Masthew at the Blue Clam. Darmo knows the mine is important to Termalaine's economy but knows little else about its history.

Getting the Quest

Speaker Masthew (lawful good half-orc veteran) explains that a group of kobolds crept into the mine a few days ago, entering from the surface and forcing the miners to abandon their jobs. He also mentions that a human miner went missing a few days before the kobolds showed up — he suspects the miner may have fallen down the central shaft.

DM Note: Masthew does not mention that he already ordered the militia to clear the mine three days ago. The militia refused for political reasons — senior officers allied with Speaker Naerth Maxildanarr of Targos are sabotaging Masthew by letting economic damage mount against him.

What's Really Going On

This quest has three layers:

  • The kobolds are refugees from Kelvin's Cairn, chased by a yeti. Their leader Trex wants safe haven in Termalaine for his tribe.
  • Janth's ghost has possessed Trex, using him to gain access to town so it can possess someone more influential. It was the ghost of human sage Janth Alowar, killed by a yeti two years ago, who is obsessed with his botanical research collection.
  • A grell invaded the mine from the Underdark via the central shaft and is the true danger — it has already killed at least one miner and is the creature the kobolds are actually fleeing from within the mine.

Approaching the Mine

The entrance is in the woods northeast of town — a 30-minute walk from Termalaine. On arrival:

The entrance to the gem mine is an open tunnel in a hillside. Empty carts are parked near the entrance, next to which a crude wooden sign has been propped up. Written on the sign in charcoal are the words "Kobolds only!" in Common.

The words are written in more elaborate calligraphy than would be expected from a kobold — a clue that something unusual is at work.

Mine Features

  • Tunnels: 7-foot ceilings. Wider chambers average 12-foot ceilings.
  • No light sources — the mine is completely dark.
  • Three levels. The deepest is Level 3.
  • A wide vertical shaft descends thousands of feet into the Underdark. It predates the mine and cannot be sealed — this is how the grell invaded.
  • Map scale: 1 square = 5 feet (Map 1.19).

Captured Kobold Intelligence

Any captured kobold freely reveals the following during interrogation:

  • "Trex led us here. He has wings like dragon!"
  • "Trex smart! He speaks like human who knows a lot. He don't speak Dragon no more."
  • "Trex says miners want their mine back. Trex wants something better in return."
  • "Trex says we be safer in town. There's food in town!"

Gem Mine — Levels 1 & 2

All areas are unlit. The mine has no light sources.

M1. Tool Room (Level 1)

Rough-hewn steps descend 60 feet from the mine entrance before ending just north of this chamber.

Racks holding picks and hammers are nailed to the walls of this small cavern. The floor is covered with rock dust and tracks.

A DC 13 Wisdom (Survival) check identifies reptilian (kobold) footprints fanning out toward M2, M3, and M4, plus giant rat tracks leading to M2 and onward to M3.

M2. Gem Deposits (Level 1)

The floor in the east end of this dusty cave is five feet higher than in the west end, with a rocky ridge separating the two areas except for slopes on either side. Picks and shovels lean against the ridge. Small gem deposits in the ridge and the walls of the cave gleam seductively.

Treasure (Mining): A character with a miner's pick who spends 1 hour chipping at the walls rolls d100:

d100Discovery
01–80Nothing
81–95Pebble-sized tourmaline (1 gp)
96–99Acorn-sized tourmaline, flawed (10 gp)
00Acorn-sized tourmaline, unflawed (100 gp)

Tourmalines found here are translucent and come in pale green, blue, pink, red, brown, and black.

M3. River Cavern (Level 1)

Characters hear rushing water before they arrive.

An underground river flows through the far side of this cavern, which is supported by a natural stone pillar near the water's edge. Between you and the river are two halfling-sized gray rodents with long tails and beady eyes. They rush to attack as soon as you notice them.

Two giant rats are here. They are aggressive and chase fleeing characters.

Underground River: Thermal vents keep this fresh-water river from freezing. It exits through a 2½-foot-wide fissure in the south wall toward M5. Any Medium or smaller creature that wades into the fissure or starts its turn there must succeed on a DC 10 Dexterity saving throw or be swept into the Underdark shaft — a fatal fall.

M4. Processing Room (Level 1)

The western tunnel from M1 drops 7 feet to reach this cavern. The ledge is easily climbed in either direction.

Benches and tables are set up as workspaces where miners clean any gemstones they find. Gravel and pebbles are strewn on the ground. Scattered across the floor are a few hammers, picks, and broken lanterns.

The miners fled in a hurry when the kobolds arrived.

Treasure: A small rock figurine of a dog with two pebble-sized tourmalines for eyes (10 gp), carved by a miner on break.

M5. Underdark Shaft — Level 1

Wooden planks and struts form a walkway along the wall of a seemingly bottomless vertical shaft. A narrow waterfall cascades down the northeast wall, and the sound of rushing water is loud in the confined space.

Characters with darkvision or a bright light source can see more walkways 30 feet below. The noise forces characters to yell to communicate.

Kobold Saboteurs: Two Icewind kobolds named Scorp and Thwip cling to the underside of the walkway, sawing through wooden struts. They need one more day to complete the sabotage. Until then the walkway is safe. Characters can spot them by looking under the planks. A kobold that takes damage while clinging falls down the shaft to its death.

M6. Bucket Lift (Level 1)

A wooden pulley system has been constructed around a large hole in the floor. A bucket big enough to hold a humanoid is held up by thick rope. Three alcoves next to the lift contain wooden boards and mining equipment.

The bucket lift travels between Level 1 (M6) and Level 2 (M7), a 15-foot descent. Capacity: 1 Medium creature or 2 Small creatures. Travel time: 1 minute up or down. Controlled by a crank inside the bucket or the crank on the pulley.

M7. Bucket Lift Landing (Level 2)

The mechanism creaks as the bucket lift descends to the floor of a small cave where two dusty tunnels lead in opposite directions.

A DC 10 Wisdom (Survival) check reveals heavy kobold foot traffic leading west, and a single set of kobold prints leading east — that kobold never returned.

M8. Grell Lair (Level 2)

A hole in the west side of this chamber opens into the central shaft, letting in the sound of the waterfall. The floor in the north end of the cave is five feet higher than in the south end, with a rocky ridge separating them. The walls above the ridge gleam with gem deposits.

A grell floats near the 15-foot ceiling. Passive Perception 16+ notices it. If more than one character enters, the grell hides. It follows the party at 30 feet, waiting to isolate and paralyze a lone target, then hauls its victim back here to devour. It drops remains down the central shaft afterward.

Treasure (Mining): Same as M2 Mining Discoveries table — roll d100 per hour of chipping above the ridge.

M9. Hungry Kobolds (Level 2)

A table and chairs are set up in this area to create a space for the miners to take breaks. On top of the table, two kobolds are poking a giant rat with their javelins to make sure it's dead. The kobolds screech loudly as they notice you.

Two Icewind kobolds named Grek and Smol attack immediately. If either is injured, both flee north toward Level 3.

Gem Mine — Level 3

M10. Underdark Shaft — Level 3

The tunnel's downward slope ends where it opens onto the center shaft. A wooden walkway extends from this opening and then runs westward to another tunnel in the rock. In front of you, a large bucket like the one you saw earlier dangles from a taut rope that stretches southward across the shaft and is connected to another wooden platform 15 feet away.

Looking down: darkness. Looking up with darkvision or bright light: the underside of the M5 walkway, 30 feet above — including Scorp and Thwip if the characters missed them earlier.

Bucket Shuttle: A rope-and-pulley system transports miners between the two platforms. Capacity: 1 Medium or 2 Small creatures. Crosses in 1 round. A creature inside can pull across by tugging the suspension rope instead of using the cranks. Dealing 5 acid, fire, or slashing damage to the rope snaps it — the bucket falls into the shaft. A character in the bucket when the rope breaks can grab a severed end with a DC 15 Dexterity save.

M11. Kobold Tunnels (Level 3)

Lurking in this dusty tunnel are three skittish kobolds. One of them carries a threadbare satchel and wears a fake pair of dragon wings made of thin wood and tattered white cloth. This kobold immediately raises its hands in surrender, saying in Common, "We mean you no harm. Please don't hurt us."

The speaker is Trex (Icewind kobold), flanked by bodyguards Vott and Zurk. Any kobolds who fled from M9 lurk behind them.

Trex's Proposition: Speaking in fluent Common, Trex explains the kobolds were chased from Kelvin's Cairn by a yeti. The miners fled before Trex could explain they meant no harm. Trex proposes: "The everlasting winter has made the wilderness unsafe for my kind, and the preternatural cold dulls our wits. Please, we only want a place to stay so we can keep out of this horrible weather. We can work, and we won't cause trouble. Termalaine would be richer for having us."

A DC 13 Wisdom (Insight) check reveals Trex is lying — the ghost controlling him wants access to Termalaine to possess someone more powerful.

Janth's Ghost

In life, Janth Alowar was a neutral human sage who catalogued Icewind Dale's flora. He and his guide were decapitated by a yeti in the foothills of Kelvin's Cairn two years ago. His restless spirit lingered, eventually possessing Trex when the kobolds sheltered during a blizzard.

The threadbare satchel Trex carries belonged to Janth. It contains clumps of moss, lichen, and shrubbery — botanical samples from Kelvin's Cairn and the tundra. None are valuable, but the ghost is possessive and will not surrender the satchel without a fight.

Laying Janth to Rest: Destroy the satchel — its last connection to the Material Plane. If the satchel is discarded without being destroyed, the ghost leaves Trex and stays near it, hoping to possess someone else. If Trex is killed, the ghost appears within 5 feet of the body and on its next turn attempts to possess a character — preferably one who can carry the satchel.

M12. Fossilized Skull and Psi Crystal (Level 3)

A fossilized skull partially juts out of the east wall of this small cave, five feet off the ground. The skull has larger than normal eye sockets, a curious ridge between the eyes, nothing that would pass for a nose, and four small holes where one would expect to see teeth.

A DC 16 Intelligence (Arcana) check identifies this as a mind flayer skull. Prying it from the stone shatters it, revealing a crystal shard where the illithid's brain would have been.

Treasure: The crystal shard is a psi crystal (Appendix D). A character can use a miner's pick to pry it free. A character who attunes to the psi crystal gains a form of indefinite madness (roll secretly on the Indefinite Madness table). While the madness lasts, the character hears a repeating telepathic distress signal from a crashed illithid ship in the mountains and can track it to its source (see "Id Ascendant" in Chapter 2). Ending attunement ends the madness and silences the signal.

M13. Geode Workface (Level 3)

Sparkling geodes greet you as you enter this cavern. The gems are partially exposed in places, sticking out from the walls like glassy shards. The floor rises near the eastern wall, leaving a natural ridge with stone ramps leading upward on either end.

Treasure: A character with a miner's pick who spends 1 hour chipping can remove an intact geode worth 10 gp. Up to 50 intact geodes can be acquired this way.

Returning to Termalaine

When the characters report back to Speaker Masthew, he thanks them and says he must inspect the mine personally before paying. He asks them to stay in Termalaine a few days while he investigates.

SituationOutcome
No kobolds, grell deadMasthew returns next day, pays 50 gp
Trex in mine, Janth's ghost not laid to restMasthew returns possessed by the ghost
Kobolds gone, grell remainsMasthew is killed by the grell; town loses its speaker
Grell still present when mine reopensMiners disappear one by one; mine closes permanently

If the kobolds are brought to Termalaine: Masthew is initially hesitant but gives them a chance. The kobolds take menial jobs and eventually win the townsfolk over. If Janth's ghost was not laid to rest, it finds a new host and Trex reverts to his skittish, unintelligent self.
